feedback.text: Beautiful Day. This is not bad, but not enough. From video games to series, there should be a law. If you buy something, then it should be possible to keep it there. The whole internet is struck and the quality of the services falls only every year. I don’t know the good solution, but I am confident that many software products will be sold to us today as a service, which should not be, and even if the company ceases to exist. Why? Because I bought it! But such half-solutions are not good because there are confusing and data-sharing consensual things. If you surf on the web, you must go through each page to refuse. The basis should be NO, this page and SE are not able to observe what I do, what I take and which pages I take. Let’s imagine Tesco would do it! It would hire people to follow and watch customers in their shops. Absurd! Then, instead of banning it completely, we nerve the user with a pop-up area for all parties. Where I have to go manually and take all the folders to NO, NO. In conclusion, my feedback is that not enough, stricter rules should protect people’s rights! Ownership of purchased goods, ownership of online material and protection for the personal sphere.
